preamble: The vocalization of the glottal fricative [h] into the high front vowel [i] is a form of lenition where the glottal constriction is lost, leaving a fronted vocalic resonance.

examples.note: Occasional vocalization of weak glottals in unstressed syllables.

sources: Catford, J. C. (1988). A Practical Introduction to Phonetics. Oxford University Press.
